Wall Street banks rule on staff betting on prediction markets, sources say
topStories.summary: In a significant move, major Wall Street banks have revised their employee conduct guidelines. The updated policies impose restrictions on engaging in financial and political prediction markets. Specifically, Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley have enacted firm prohibitions, while JPMorgan Chase and Bank of America have also established comparable limitations. These initiatives are designed to eliminate conflicts of interest and maintain compliance with industry standards.

topStories.title: SpaceX's near-term AI payoff seen tethered to Earth, not outer space
topStories.summary: SpaceX's current value stems from building essential AI infrastructure on Earth. The company is generating significant revenue from terrestrial compute contracts with major clients. These deals are expected to far exceed income from other SpaceX businesses this year. Orbital AI remains a longer-term prospect dependent on technological advancements. Analysts believe terrestrial compute will drive SpaceX's growth for the remainder of this decade.

topStories.title: Oil heads for weekly gain as Middle East supply risks persist
topStories.summary: On Friday, oil prices saw a modest increase as they aimed for weekly gains. Renewed hostilities between the US and Iran have severely limited shipping activity in the Strait of Hormuz, leading to a virtual halt in tanker traffic. This disruption followed Iran's burial of its late supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Despite this, market confidence in diplomatic efforts appears to be restraining oil price surges.

india.title: It has bright yellow wings and looks familiar, but this rare British butterfly is unlike any other, DNA reveals
india.summary: A new study confirms the British swallowtail butterfly is genetically unique. This native species possesses significantly less genetic diversity than European relatives. Researchers found no accumulation of harmful mutations despite its isolation. However, higher inbreeding levels pose challenges for its long-term survival. Genomic research offers vital insights for protecting vulnerable butterfly populations.

business.title: Wall Street banks rule on staff betting on prediction markets, sources say

The updated policies impose restrictions on engaging in financial and political prediction markets. Specifically, Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley have enacted firm prohibitions, while JPMorgan Chase and Bank of America have also established comparable limitations. These initiatives are designed to eliminate conflicts of interest and maintain compliance with industry standards.

Analysts believe terrestrial compute will drive SpaceX's growth for the remainder of this decade.
